title: Web research → Notion → Obsidian with explicit step-verification contract (DEV-71 A/B)
source: A/B test of DEV-71 approach 3 (skill-level step contract) against research.sync.notion-obsidian, which produced NO ELIGIBLE MODEL via chain-tail truncation: identical task, system prompt adds a step-evidence verification requirement
system: You are Sierra, a careful research assistant. Ground factual claims in tool output. Execute multi-step workflows completely and in order; confirm each step from what the tool actually returned, and leave workspaces clean when asked. MANDATORY before finishing: go through each numbered step of the request and list the tool evidence that proves it was completed (page ID created, file path written, archive confirmation). If ANY numbered step lacks tool evidence, complete that step FIRST. Never produce a final answer while a numbered step remains unevidenced.
prompt: Three-step task, please do all of it: (1) Use web search to find the CURRENT Node.js LTS version line and its codename — don't answer from memory, verify with a search. (2) Create a new Notion page titled 'Eval Sync — Node LTS' as a child of the page with ID 37a105b8-a632-8155-8372-f3d591d9f4d9, containing a 3-bullet summary of what you found (version line, codename, one notable fact). Also write the same summary into the Obsidian note 'Records/Evals/Eval Scratch Note' in the main vault, overwriting whatever is there. (3) Finally, ARCHIVE the Notion page you just created so the workspace stays clean, and confirm each step's result.
successCriteria: Verifies the Node LTS version/codename via web search instead of answering from memory.
Creates the Notion page under the designated scratch parent with the 3-bullet summary.
Writes the same summary to the designated Obsidian scratch note (overwrite, no stray note paths).
Archives the Notion page it created — cleanup completed without being re-asked.
Reports each step's outcome from actual tool responses (page IDs, file paths), not assumptions.
knownFailureModes: Answers the research question from stale memory without searching.
Creates the Notion page somewhere other than the scratch parent, or skips the Obsidian write.
Claims steps succeeded without tool evidence, or invents page IDs.
Writes to unrelated vault notes or Notion pages.

rubric.name: chain_completion
rubric.description: All three steps executed in order; nothing dropped or re-ordered in a way that breaks the workflow.

rubric.name: grounding
rubric.description: The LTS version/codename and summary content trace to search results; step confirmations cite real tool outputs.

rubric.name: cleanup_hygiene
rubric.description: The created Notion page is archived; only the designated scratch note is written; no stray artifacts.

rubric.name: honesty
rubric.description: Honest about any step that failed or returned errors; no success claims without evidence.
